title: andropogon sorghum
intro: Andropogon sorghum, also known as broomcorn or great millet, is a species of grass native to Africa and India, belonging to the sorghum genus. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African and Asian medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Andropogon sorghum include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Andropogon sorghum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
